notes: ActionTypeID is the trade-action enum on Fact_CustomerAction. Customer-and-identity has the lookup table but routing goes to trading.
Airdrop = promo crypto distribution (marketing channel). Staking/trading drop bare.
Apex = US broker. Canonical recon in domain-cross. Trading-via-Apex stays in domain-options via 'apex options' qualified form.
Apex Start-of-Day reconciliation. Lives in domain-cross/provider-reconciliation.md.
Appropriateness test = pre-trade KYC quiz; customer-and-identity primary. Options keeps 'options appropriateness' qualified.

Two legitimate angles: (a) tribe-emoney-audit (cross-domain audit trail) -> domain-cross; (b) Fact_CustomerAction history (per-customer activity audit) -> domain-customer-and-identity. CONTRACT: 'audit trail for [transaction|emoney|broker recon]' -> cross; 'audit trail for [customer|accountid|history]' -> customer-and-identity.
Dedicated hub for AUM/AUA. Niche platforms keep qualified forms ('moneyfarm AUM', 'spaceship AUM').
First-5-actions = early lifecycle table; customer-and-identity owns.
C2F end-to-end story lives in domain-cross/crypto-to-fiat.md. Secondaries keep qualified forms ('C2F revenue', 'C2F leg').
Crypto-to-Platform = trading deposit via crypto. Payments primary.
Refund-chargeback chain lives in domain-cross/refund-chargeback-chain.md.
Bare 'commission' = trading commission. Marketing must qualify ('affiliate commission', 'partner commission').
CorrelationId is the EXW chain-stitch column; only meaningful in wallet context.
Bare 'crypto' = instrument category. Payments uses qualified forms ('crypto deposit', 'crypto wallet').
DMA = direct mail attribution channel; marketing primary.
EquityGlobal = TP-side AUM column.
ETH as instrument. Staking keeps 'ETH staking' qualified form.
EXW_C2F_E2E table is the canonical C2F join; lives in domain-cross.
EXW_DimUser = wallet user dimension; lives in domain-exw-wallet.
Fact_CurrencyPriceWithSplit is the global FX-price table; trading owns. Niche platforms reference it as input but drop as trigger.
Fact_CustomerAction is the trade fact. Customer-and-identity uses it for lifecycle - drops as trigger.

FTD = first time deposit = customer lifecycle event. Payments has the deposit event; rev has the FTD-revenue marker. Both keep qualified forms ('FTD revenue', 'FTD deposit table'). Bare FTD goes to lifecycle owner.
PHASE 5 ADDITION: 'funded accounts' is currently NOT a trigger on customer-populations-and-lifecycle.md. Must be ADDED. Spaceship keeps 'spaceship funded accounts' qualified form.
Gatsby = same US broker (legacy name). Same as Apex.
Phrase form of IsGlobalFTD. Added in Phase 5 to customer-populations-and-lifecycle.md; was previously only claimed by domain-payments/mimo-panel-and-ddr.md (removed in follow-up cleanup).

Niche-platform flag column. Spaceship uses for SPS FTD detection; moneyfarm uses same column name on MF tables. CONTRACT: 'is_ftd spaceship/SPS' -> spaceship; 'is_ftd moneyfarm/MF' -> moneyfarm; bare 'is_ftd' is too generic - re-rank by platform mention or required_tables.
Same shape as is_ftd; routing depends on platform mention.
Options + spaceship both have this flag. Context: 'is_internal_transfer options' -> options; bare or 'is_internal_transfer spaceship' -> spaceship via 'spaceship' qualifier.
IsCreditReportValidCB = global valid-customer-fingerprint filter; lives in cross-cutting.
IsCryptoToFiat flag on Fact_MIMO_AllPlatforms - C2F marker.
IsGlobalFTD = lifecycle FTD across all platforms; lives in customer-populations-and-lifecycle.md.
CamelCase form of is_internal_transfer; options + payments both claim. Payments drops bare - keeps qualified ('IsInternalTransfer payment').
Recurring deposit flag - lives in recurring-deposit-to-trade story.
Global valid-customer macro.
MCP latency signal lives in cross-cutting (mcp-latency-signal rule).
Payment-processor Merchant Identifier.
MirrorID = copy-trading mirror identifier.
moneyfarmUserId is the MF-side identifier; MF hub owns.
Bare 'net deposits' = global NTD on the trading platform. Niche platforms must use qualified form ('spaceship net deposits'). revenue-and-fees has NTD as input to revenue calc - drops bare.
Funnel = ops/onboarding KPI; customer-and-identity has populations but drops the funnel term.
Niche-platform-scoped FTD - belongs to the niche hub by definition.
Niche-platform-scoped revenue belongs to the niche hub.
PlayerStatus = lifecycle enum on Dim_Customer. Compliance has KYC-flow scope, ops has onboarding scope - both keep 'PlayerStatus KYC' / 'PlayerStatus onboarding' qualified.
Popular Investor program lives in trading. Customer-and-identity has profile fields - drops as trigger.
Bare 'reconciliation' = broker/SOD recon (domain-cross). Trading drops bare, keeps 'trade reconciliation' qualified. Staking drops bare.
ScreeningStatus = AML/KYC outcome - compliance is primary, NOT customer-and-identity.
Simplex = card-purchase-of-crypto provider; payment-flow primary.
CopyPortfolio product (called Smart Portfolio in UI). Trading-side product.
SOD recon table family - cross-broker scope.
Tangany = custodian for crypto wallet.
Treezor = eMoney IBAN provider.
USABroker = same US broker (alt name).
VerificationLevelID = KYC level - compliance primary.
